content.problemStatement:
In this example, we will demonstrate how seekg is used to move the read pointer to a specific location in a file (example.txt) and read from that position.

- seekg is used to set the file pointer to a specific position for reading.
- We use seekg(position) to move the pointer to the specified byte offset.

**In this case, we are moving the pointer to the 5th position, so 'i' will be printed. Check the contents of example.txt.**
